title: The Phenomenon of Man
wikitext:
[]: The Phenomenon of Man             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Jan 04, 2013 11:09 pm This is a book named The Phenomenon of Man: [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Phenomenon_of_Man[/url] Why it is noted here is clear. Mr. Teilhard's work, with the focus on the continued evolution of humanity and the coming existence of a fused and consuming universal conciousness as a thinking layer that envelops the earth, is bloody Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Jan 05, 2013 12:52 am That definitely looks like an interesting read. I'll add it to my list of things to read. My long, long, very long, long list of things to read....

[]: Re: The Phenomenon of Man             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 05, 2013 1:40 am Lavinius wrote: Why it is noted here is clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jan 05, 2013 1:14 pm So, is this a thread where we yell at Anno for stealing ideas? Or, better yet, is it a thread where... Actually, what are we discussing here? Just the book in general? Of course, this requires that we read the book first. Hm. Is it long?

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Jan 05, 2013 9:56 pm That isn't exactly clear to me, what this thread is about. I just thought the similiarities were worth noting. Maybe something in the book (I believe it to be online, on good foundations) could help with an Instrumentality discussion or something... There isn't really a good reason for this thread to be open (though I think its existence is wise.) Now that the book has been noted, it can be locked.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jan 05, 2013 11:25 pm Let's wait until after people have read the book to do that. This thread could have potential.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jan 06, 2013 5:34 pm Having read the book, I honestly don't see how it's relevant at all. Omega Point sort of resembles NemZ's interpretation of Gendo/TV End, but it doesn't match up to the general interpretation of "all minds becoming one."

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 06, 2013 6:14 pm The thinking reflects part of the broader inter-war zeitgeist -- similar notions surfaced in speculative fiction of the time, such as Olaf Stapledon's grand cosmic histories Last and First Men and Star Maker , where hierarchies of mind are built up into a universal consciousness.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Jan 06, 2013 8:16 pm AuraTwilight wrote: it doesn't match up to the general interpretation of "all minds becoming one."

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jan 07, 2013 4:14 pm Do you mean to say either that it doesn't match up to the statement that "all minds become one", or that Mr. Teilhard's interpretation is not the same as Eva's (or your, or the common) one? I'll assume the second, as the first seems ridiculous.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 4:54 pm I disagree with you on that point. In instrumentality, you are however you perceive yourself to be. This in and of itself is a form of self-awareness. People know who they are, but they are simply a part of a much larger collective. This was illustrated during Episode 25-26.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Jan 07, 2013 5:16 pm Although, it could be argued that Instrumentality never completed.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 6:34 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Although, it could be argued that Instrumentality never completed.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Jan 07, 2013 6:44 pm Everyone has their individuality because Shinji rejects Instrumentality. Alternatively, Instrumentality is averted by Gendou and Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 6:47 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Everyone has their individuality because Shinji rejects Instrumentality. Alternatively, Instrumentality is averted by Gendou and Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jan 07, 2013 7:02 pm Rei: The LCL Sea... Within the sea where life began. A world without AT Fields... without your own shape. An ambiguous world where it is impossible to tell where you end and other people start. A fragile world where you exist everywhere, and thus exist nowhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 7:04 pm I'm talking about EvaTV. I've got a whole other list of information pertaining to EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jan 07, 2013 7:10 pm Shinji: This is... A space with nothing in it. A world with nothing in it. A world that has nothing else in it, except me. I'm losing my grasp on who I am. I feel like I'm disappearing. The existence that is me is vanishing.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 7:12 pm That isn't what he's saying during the sequence that I've mentioned in question. It's a female off screen voice, telling Shinji about how much more comfortable he feels with restrictions.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jan 07, 2013 7:13 pm He said that after the sequence you're referring to. Actually, the script makes it look like it's at the end of that particular sequence.
